UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and their registration details for a service package within the AuraFit application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically mirrors the authenticated user's ID.

fullName.description: The full name of the user as provided during registration.

email.description: The email address of the user.

allergies.description: Details about any food allergies the user has. This is part of their health profile.

injuryHistory.description: Details about the user's past or current injuries. This is part of their health profile.

subscriptionStatus.description: The current status of the user's package subscription (e.g., 'pending', 'active', 'cancelled').

packageId.description: Reference to the Package the user has registered for. (Relationship: Package 1:N UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

Package.title: Package
Package.description: Defines an exercise package offered by AuraFit, including its details, pricing, and features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Package entity.

name.description: The name of the exercise package (e.g., 'Beginner's Boost', 'Advanced Transformation').

description.description: A detailed description of what the package includes and its benefits.

price.description: The monetary cost of the package.

features.description: A list of key features or benefits included in the package.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the package for display in UI, especially for floating cards.

isActive.description: Indicates if the package is currently available for registration or display.

ResultGalleryItem.title: ResultGalleryItem
ResultGalleryItem.description: Represents a 'Before and After' transformation entry for the Result Gallery section.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ResultGalleryItem entity.

clientName.description: The name or pseudonym of the client whose results are displayed.

beforeImageUrl.description: URL to the 'before' image of the client transformation.

afterImageUrl.description: URL to the 'after' image of the client transformation.

testimonial.description: A brief testimonial or description of the client's journey and results.

displayOrder.description: An optional number to control the display order of items in the gallery.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this result was published.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is strictly restricted to the authenticated user matching the `{userId}`. This includes the `health_profile` map (containing `allergies` and `injuryHistory`) and `subscriptionStatus`. Includes denormalized 'health_profile' map for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user, which is also the document ID.

definition.description: Stores publicly viewable exercise packages offered by AuraFit. All documents in this collection are publicly readable.

params.name: packageId
params.description: The unique identifier for an exercise package.

definition.description: Stores publicly viewable 'Before and After' transformation results for the gallery. All documents in this collection are publicly readable.

params.name: galleryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a result gallery item.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles. It leverages structural segregation and path-based authorization to simplify security rules and ensure Authorization Independence and support for QAPs (Query As Permissions).

1.  **Authorization Independence & Denormalization:**
    *   **`/users/{userId}`**: Each user's profile is stored in a document whose ID directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID. This means authorization for a user's profile (`read`, `write`) depends solely on `request.auth.uid == userId` and the document itself. All relevant user-specific data, including the `health_profile` map with `allergies` and `injuryHistory`, `subscriptionStatus`, etc., is contained within this single document. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document attributes or other collection data, thereby maintaining Authorization Independence and supporting atomic operations.
    *   **`/packages/{packageId}`** and **`/resultGalleryItems/{galleryItemId}`**: These collections contain public, read-only data. Their authorization is inherently independent as they do not rely on user-specific roles or related documents. Any user (authenticated or not) can read them, making their security rules extremely simple (`allow read: true`).

2.  **QAPs (Query As Permissions):**
    *   **`/users/{userId}`**: For user profiles, `list` operations on the `/users` collection are explicitly disallowed or restricted to very specific, highly privileged roles (e.g., `admin` if such a role were implemented, which would be in a separate `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection). Regular users are expected to fetch their own profile directly using their `uid` (e.g., `doc('users', request.auth.uid)`), which is a secure, direct access pattern. This prevents users from querying the entire collection and inadvertently exposing data, thereby enforcing QAPs by design.
    *   **`/packages/{packageId}`** and **`/resultGalleryItems/{galleryItemId}`**: For these public collections, `list` operations are explicitly allowed for all users. This is secure because the data contained within is intentionally public information for displaying the application's services and results. The security rule `allow read: true;` for these collections directly supports secure QAPs for listing, as no sensitive data is exposed.

3.  **Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`users`, `packages`, `resultGalleryItems`) is designed to contain documents that share a consistent security posture. `/users/{userId}` documents are private to the owning user. `/packages/{packageId}` and `/resultGalleryItems/{galleryItemId}` documents are publicly readable. This segregation prevents mixing different security requirements within the same collection, making security rules much simpler and easier to audit.

4.  **Access Modeling & Consistency:** Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}`) is used for private user data, ensuring that the owner's UID is an explicit part of the document's path. Consistent and descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{userId}`, `{packageId}`, `{galleryItemId}`) are used for clarity.
